zh.lifeSummary: Jerome Pyce Pease於1584年出生在英國英格蘭素瑞尤厄爾。他於大約1608年與Alice Glover Stephens在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1682年3月3日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可享年98歲。

nameMeanings.name: Pease
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English pese, pise (Old English peose, pise) ‘pea’. The word was commonly used to denote something small or of little worth, as in the phrase not worth a pese. It may have been used to nickname someone who habitually used the term or who was thought to merit it. It might alternatively have been a name for a pea-seller. Compare Bean .
Americanized form of French Pié (see Pie ), with the addition of excrescent -s, a common feature of Americanized surnames.

History: Robert and John Pease came from Great Baddow, Essex, England, to Salem, MA, in 1634. In 1644 Robert died, leaving a son (also called Robert) who was apprenticed as a weaver in Salem. By 1646 John Pease was living on Martha's Vineyard.

variants.name: Bean
meanings: English: nickname for a pleasant person, from Middle English bēne ‘friendly, amiable’.

English: metonymic occupational name for a grower or seller of beans, from Middle English bene ‘bean’ (Old English bēan ‘beans’, a collective singular). The broad bean, Vicia faba, was a staple food in Europe in the Middle Ages. The green bean, Phaseolus vulgaris, came from South America and was not introduced to Europe until the late 16th century. The word bene was commonly used to denote something of little worth, and occasionally it may have been applied as a nickname for someone considered insignificant.

English: possibly a habitational or topographic name. Redmonds, Dictionary of Yorkshire Surnames, cites Adam del Bene of Harrogate (1351) as evidence to suggest that in the Harrogate area, where the Yorkshire name later proliferated, it may have been derived from a place where beans grew.

nameMeanings.name: Jerome
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Vernacular form of the Greek name Hieronymos, derived from hieros ‘holy’ + onoma ‘name’. St Jerome ( c.342–420 ) was a citizen of the Eastern Roman Empire, who bore the Greek names Eusebios Hieronymos Sophronios; he was chiefly responsible for the translation into Latin of the Bible, the Vulgate. He also wrote many works of commentary and exposition on the Bible, and is regarded as one of the Doctors of the Church. The Greek form of the name was used occasionally in England; it is recorded in Nottinghamshire, for example, in the late 16th century. Both Jerome and Jeronimus are found in Yorkshire and elsewhere from that date onwards. The name was borne by the British writer Jerome K. Jerome ( 1859–1927 ), the American songwriter Jerome Kern ( 1885–1945 ), and the American ballet dancer and choreographer Jerome Robbins ( 1918–98 ).

nameMeanings.name: Pyce
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Pine  . This name has been established in Ireland since the 16th century.

en.lifeSummary: Jerome Pyce Pease was born in 1584, in Ewell, Surrey, England, United Kingdom. He married Alice Glover Stephens about 1608, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died on 3 March 1682, in Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 98.

ko.Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Briti: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크
ko.lifeSummary: Jerome Pyce Pease(은)는 1584년 영국 잉글랜드 수리 웰에서 태어났다. 그는 대략 1608년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Alice Glover Stephens(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1682년 3월 3일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크에서 98세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Briti: суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Jerome Pyce Pease родился в 1584 г. в местности ювелл, сурри, Англия, Великобритания. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Glover Stephens около 1608 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер 3 марта 1682 г. в местности суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 98 лет.
